reviews.review_text:
It was my first forza game, so i have a very strong sentimental value to it. 
It has it short commings? yes.
i've made great memories with it? hell yes

its great to play with friends

As much as it is fun to casually just drive around the map with a wheel Forza Horizon 5 feels boring and like a chore to play.

Progression doesn't exist. You turn the wheel? EXP. You stare at a tree? EXP. You fart? The game aggressively throws three Bugattis and a million credits at your face.

The map is beautiful but roads are just suggestions. You can just tape down the throttle and bulldoze straight through the entire ecosystem at 200 mph.

You spend two hours in the garage tuning your absolute dream car. You pull up to the starting line, pumped and ready to unleash hell. The game: "Ah, excuse me, no. This is a 'Sensible Footwear' event. You are strictly required to drive a factory-standard 2014 Ford Focus."
I get that restrictions exist to make races fair but in a sandbox game supposedly built around limitless freedom, nothing hurts more than the game confiscating your custom built monster and forcing you to drive a commuter hatchback.

The menu is so cluttered with promotion and nonsense it takes 10 minutes to navigate an unholy labyrinth of pop-up ads, seasonal promos and wheelspins just to find your garage and change a tire. This game is expensive and I don't feel like I got even close to money worth from this game.

The progression is non existent, you get spammed with super cars right off the bat. The map is pretty boring, it's practically an entire desert with low variety in environments. However the gameplay is pretty fun and is the best sim-cade racer on the market in terms of gameplay (at least until Forza Horizon 6 releases)

Forza Horizon 5 is easily the best racing game I’ve played. I originally picked it up as something casual to play between bigger, story-driven games, but it quickly became one of the games I spent the most time with.

What surprised me the most was the sheer amount of content. There’s always something to do, whether it’s racing, exploring, collecting cars, or just driving around. More importantly, the content feels varied and well-made rather than being there just for the sake of quantity.

My only real criticism is the progression and reward system. The game is extremely generous, to the point where you’ll end up with a huge collection of cars almost immediately. Before you have time to appreciate one car, you’ve already unlocked several more. While it’s fun at first, it made the cars feel less special and took away some of the sense of progression that I usually enjoy in racing games.

Even with that flaw, Forza Horizon 5 is an incredible experience and a game I kept coming back to for hundreds of hours.

Generally a downgrade in some aspects over FH4, but enjoyable nonetheless. Still one of the best casual racing games out there. Good optimization, beautiful (especially in HDR) and the races are plenty of fun.

Overall, I think Forza Horizon is a really enjoyable game that can keep you entertained for a very long time. The cars feel fantastic to drive, and the individual races are a lot of fun — especially the off-road tracks, where you can really feel the terrain. The selection of cars is also huge, and there are plenty of customization options. The game world is varied (forests, deserts, mountains, cities, etc.) and fun to explore.

However, there are also a few downsides, which I’d like to list below:

1) The requirement to have a Microsoft account, the unnecessary and out-of-place casino-style wheel-spin UI when winning new cars, and the challenge cards prominently displayed on the map that cannot be hidden — which are not part of the base game (and must therefore be purchased first) — really rubbed me the wrong way.
2) The constant chatter of the unlikable characters against an overly colorful backdrop sometimes makes the game world feel pretty generic. As for the radio stations, I often wished there were more customization options because the music selection just wasn’t really my thing. I often preferred to just turn the radio off.
3) As much fun as it is to drive around, the game really should have a free fast-travel option on a map this big. And when it comes to starting a race, you still have to sit through several layers of loading and cutscenes that I really wish I could skip more often.
4) The game constantly tries to push you toward certain activities (for example by repeatedly opening the mission map), even though the game is supposed to be all about player freedom. Some level of guidance is fine, but this kind of constant nudging really got on my nerves.
5) There was no German voiceover for the Donut missions. There’s also a strange vehicle-related glitch that I couldn’t explain. Sometimes the car just refuses to accelerate past a certain speed, or it won’t move forward at all, even though the road is flat with no obstacles and the car was previously at a standstill.
6) Some challenges/races seemed impossible for me to complete. For example, you're supposed to reach a certain speed on a stretch of beach within a set time limit, but the car suddenly refuses to accelerate any further at that spot, no matter what you do. Elsewhere, certain opponents gain all sorts of advantages, such as sudden, extreme speed boosts out of nowhere, or the ability to take shortcuts or navigate even the tightest turns without slowing down, while your own vehicle gets artificially slowed down. I found that kind of thing frustrating.

“Bigger map, brighter world, same incredible driving fun.”

Forza Horizon 5 takes everything great about the series and pushes it into a more vibrant, more diverse, and more visually impressive direction. Set in a stunning recreation of Mexico, the game immediately stands out with its color, scale, and environmental variety.

The world design is easily one of its biggest strengths. From dense jungles and sandy deserts to volcanic peaks and lively cities, every region feels distinct and worth exploring. The dynamic weather system and seasonal events add extra life without overwhelming the experience.

Driving feels as smooth and satisfying as ever. The handling is accessible but still rewarding, striking a nice balance between arcade fun and light realism. Whether you’re racing competitively, drifting for style points, or just cruising around, the game consistently feels good to play.

There’s also an overwhelming amount of content. Events, stories, online modes, seasonal playlists, and car collections ensure there’s always something to chase. It’s the kind of game you can drop into for 20 minutes or lose an entire evening in without realizing.

However, compared to its predecessor, the core gameplay loop doesn’t feel dramatically different. It’s more of an evolution than a revolution. Some players may also find the progression a bit too relaxed, and online stability can occasionally be inconsistent.

Pros:

Stunning and diverse open world
Extremely smooth and fun driving mechanics
Huge amount of content and events
Excellent visual presentation

Cons:

Feels similar to FH4 in core structure
Progression can feel too easy
Occasional online/server issues

Verdict:
A visually spectacular and consistently fun racing experience. It doesn’t reinvent the formula, but it absolutely perfects it.

Highly Recommended 👍

Unfortunately I paid full price for this game. Definitely not worth it and I wish I refunded it at the time, but I hoped it would get better, it did not. Bland, soulless, lifeless and every other synonym you can think of. Between playing horizon 4 and 6 they totally missed the mark with 5. Best to avoid this one.

this game is fun and recommendable to others but the price is alot but the game itself is nice

I have to say this one was a very bad, almost every aspect of this game is terrible except the visual aspect and even that was largely brought over from 4. 

The worst parts are easily the Eliminator and the map, that mode is chock full of hackers and cheaters whilst the map seems like it was designed specifically to make you want to castrate yourself for even trying to enjoy the eliminator, the normal races don't really suffer that much although they are very boring since they lack any complexity whatsoever leading to large long stretches of straight roads with the occasional right turn, don't buy this and go play either 4 or 6.
I really want to like this game but every other time I try to open the game I get a "sign in needed" without any way to sign in.

As we're heading to Horizon Festival's next destination, I feel like I should say a few words about this game before hopping behind the wheel of my Lancer VI for the last time in Mexico. 

I made acquaintance with the Forza series from Forza Motorsport 4 on my second-hand Xbox360 back in the day. It was brilliant. Hugging apexes in roaring Ferraris, drifting high in the alps in JDM classics - it was new, fresh, and bloody well done. 

The same feeling carried over to Horizon. Road racing, hugging tight corners with your loyal beast purring underneath you, pulling insane G's. Drifting, late night sessions with the boys, pushing each other into walls because none of you knew how to properly drift. With an addition of... offroad (excluding Horizon 1), exploring the map in 1,000 horsepower buggies and SUV's while listening to fast-paced Drum & Bass. Playground Games did a damn good job with Horizon 3 and 4, being my all-time favorite Forza games. 

But Horizon 5? There are good and bad things about it. Can't complain, overall experience was amazing. Heart-stopping near misses, clean and dirty overtakes, PvP and PvE racing with little kids trying to push you off the track or ram you into walls - it was fun, generally.

Generally.

My main complaint is how barren the map feels. Don't get me wrong, I have countless laps around the whole map of Mexico in my Lancer, with tight corners and long straights diversifying the experience. It's just that the middle of the map feels empty. Same feel, despite where you drive. Vast fields, tight roads, little huts scattered around the land. It was not enough. It still felt empty and uneventful. As if the whole map was designed around the Battle Royale mode, which, let's admit it, was a flop. 

The City.
It felt rural. For a major city on the map it felt just ancient. Empty. Small. Suffice it to say that after completing every single race in that area I never even considered driving around the city [i] just because [/i]. Simple as that. I have high hopes for Tokyo, and I am very excited to drift through the neon-filled streets of a MODERN city, just like in Horizon 3. 

Car Sounds.

Now, pardon me. This section is going to be pretty short, as I have just one major complaint. Why in the ♥♥♥♥ are they re-using sounds for the same car brand over and over again? Lambos and Ferraris sound the EXACT same no matter the model.

Overall..

The game is great. Late-night drives solo or with friends, competing for the first place with randoms whose ego urges them to push you off the course, custom-tuning your cars to your liking, racing through the vast landscapes with Hospital Records blasting fast-paced tunes in your ear and the engine of your AMG GT R roaring like a wild beast beneath you - all those granted me plenty of good memories, fun moments and an overall great impression of the game despite its flaws.

Rating: 7.5/10. 

I'll see you in Japan, fellow drivers. Let's race, clean and dirty, for one to emerge victorious!

This game is just so... soulless. The map, while big, isn't astonishing nor varied in colors and seasons do pretty much nothing. It's pretty much dry in 50 flavors. I should also add, that the Horizon Pulse soundtrack is just plain bad, but since I like pop, there is really nothing much else I can listen to. It is not a bad game, you will most probably enjoy it to some extent, but it lacks in comparison with both its predecessor and successor, and I simply cannot recommend it.
Boring map, boring progression, bad story, just overall boring. Racing is fun and it runs very well, but that's all there is to it. Buy Horizon 4 or 6 instead.
Slow loading times, awkward interaction between steam deck and playing on PC and trouble with using a steering wheel.

game is very nice and has really good physics and graphics.
only bad thing is that microsoft ♥♥♥♥♥♥ it up with the terrible sign in system that doesnt work for ♥♥♥♥. they should fix it.
no longer able to open the game because microsoft requires me to sign in and refuses to continue in the sign in window. cannot recommend a game that I can no longer play.

If you are running on Windows 11 with the latest drivers don't waste your time. The game will automatically flag an error with DirectX saying your graphics are not supported but all my drivers are in fact compatible for running the game. I tried searching online for a workaround but everything involved uninstalling my drivers, reinstalling them, uninstalling the game, reinstalling the game and then MAYBE the game would work

I loved FH4, so I thought I'd enjoy the next installment, right? Wrong. This garbage was so hammy I couldn't even enjoy driving around. It felt infuriating to play because the cliché Latin announcer/characters were a total turn-off from a car culture that could've been represented better. The landscape was ridiculous, from misplaced native ruins in a spit of a jungle oasis to miles of depressing arid desert. It's like the developer took the extremes of the geography and mashed it all into a non-sense map. I was also bummed that after years from the FH4 release, the graphics barely progressed besides adding a few third-party features developed in FH4 mods.

The cars were fine. Nothing spectacular. The cars were just fine.

I’ve been playing Forza Horizon 5 for a while now, and honestly it’s one of the most enjoyable racing games I’ve played. What I like the most is how easy it is to make money and collect cars. The game keeps rewarding you with credits, wheelspins, and free cars, so it never feels too grindy. Even after a few hours of playing, my garage was already full of amazing cars.

The map looks beautiful and there’s always something fun to do, whether it’s racing, exploring, or just driving around. It’s also a game that pretty much anyone can enjoy. Kids can have fun with the simple controls and colorful world, while adults can enjoy the huge variety of cars and events.

The only part I found difficult was drifting. It definitely takes practice to get the timing and control right, especially during drift zones. At first I kept spinning out, but after some time it became really satisfying.

Overall, Forza Horizon 5 is a super fun game with great graphics, lots of content, and a rewarding progression system. Definitely worth playing if you enjoy racing games.

One of the best open world racing games I’ve played since Need For Speed Most Wanted (2005)! Each car feels unique and handles differently with a pretty solid amount of customization available, and no shortage of options on cars to choose from.

The story is basic and barebones, but what racing game actually has a decent story anyways?

I do wish there was more cosmetic customization, such as more body kits, spoilers, etc. Majority of the cosmetic customizations come from paint jobs (where you can either create your own or download and use one created by the community)

The worst part about Forza Horizon 5 is The Eliminator. I’m not a fan of Battle Royale modes to begin with, but this is by far one of the WORST versions of a BR I’ve played. Thankfully The Eliminator is entirely optional unless you’re an achievement hunter like myself.

Easily recommend to fans of racing games and car enthusiasts

The game is fine it's more that the Drivatars are coded in such a lobotomized way that does not make any sense. When somebody has high fps the AI starts to drive in such a way that it makes it very difficult to beat, regardless of low or high difficulty. The only way I found a way to make the game make any sense physics and AI driver wise, is to lower my fps to 30 then the AI behaves normally. I found that annoying and it makes the game either too easy or unbeatable, without an in-between.

The game is constantly giving you ADs for either the DLC for this game, or asking you to buy the next game: Forza Horizon 6. Despite Steam's rules saying NO ADs! It's incredibly annoying. Pretty much after every race you get an AD of some kind.

After spending more than 71 hours of playtime, as shown in the review image image_65e5b1.png, I can confidently say that Forza Horizon 5 is the ultimate masterpiece in racing games. I highly recommend it to everyone without hesitation! The game delivers an exceptional and breathtaking experience in terms of realism and mind-blowing graphics. The stunning attention to car details and the vibrant open-world of Mexico look completely lifelike. The overall gameplay is incredibly fun and diverse, seamlessly blending pure driving enjoyment with responsive, precise handling physics. What truly sets this game apart is its massive variety of activities, including thrilling races, drag racing to test your top speeds and custom fine-tuned configurations, and incredible off-roading across rugged terrains and desert dunes. On top of that, the online multiplayer lets you easily cruise, team up, and compete with friends without a single dull moment.
love the game fun to play with family and friends love this game and we made the right game cool
Boring as hell. You spend more time looking at advertisements and bright cutscenes than actually playing. Too much visual garbage on the screen.
A somewhat boring map makes this the weakest of the series IMO but still an excellent game. Performance is excellent, playing buttery smooth at 4k 120fps.
It is one of the most beautiful feelings you can ever experience. I wish everyone could have this game <3

It just plays itself. Theres no challenge, no progression, no intrigue. I have amazing cars from the start, everyone loves me and theres no reason to play the game. Boring. I'm not going to watch the refund window expire while just going through the motions.

Had a bit of difficulty getting it to run on (Fedora) Linux.  Once in, it (appears to at least) have you forced into a multiplayer online mode where driving around the world there are other human players which get in your way.  There is also a lot of complexity with different cars, a bunch of difference ways you have to "buy" different things.  This is NOT a game for casual gamers.  Be prepared to dump hours into the game just to get started.  Some people love it, but it wasn't for me.

Wasn't sure if I would like it, but actually it's easy to pick up and have a bit of fun. Nice big open world, lots of side missions, loads of different racing disciplines, great graphics and a huge array of vehicles. If you want realism, as in simulator quality vehicle dynamics stay well clear.

This is my first Forza game. I first played on Game pass with a month free trial in Dec 2024. My original intention was to play another game. Luckily on Steam this game went on Sale, so I used my Steam Gift Card I got for Christmas that year and started downloading the game on Boxing Day (that is the day after Christmas Day for non-UK resident gamers).

I was instantly hooked on this sand box game driving everywhere, collecting everything. Impressed by the range of cars available. Able to do things in an order I chose.

I am aware people say this is their least favourite map, but I have only 4 (which I purchase in a sale before it’s delisting then forgot about), and 6 to compare to. I love the long stretch of road in the middle of the map while the fast cars in my collection. The flatter lands also mean less obstacles so I can just tear across the landscape (I have driven along every road to).

So yes i recommend this game.

But you can play it on pc or laptop. GeForce now is a app to play your games anywhere but you need wifi.

This is a game that I have really mixed feelings about. Having only played the first 2 entries in this series on the xbox 360, coming back to the franchise via this game feels weird. 

My biggest disappointment is the map, which compared to the previous Horizon games feels very repetitive and empty. There is almost no elevation change in the whole map so you can full throttle in the off road across the whole map without any thought. The only memorable of the base game map is the volcano, which had that long drift zone that was very enjoyable to drift through. 

Another aspect of the game I disliked is the superstar treatment you get in the festival from the start. Arriving in a new location, immediately being treated like a celebrity and being gifted the fastest cars feels very weird. I really wish the game had you start out as an unknown driver, working your way to that celebrity status.

Even with all of this, it is still a fun game, with really good graphics and very good optimization. The driving feels really good, and the car roster is massive. Overall I think this game is worth buying now only if you can catch the game on sale with a big discount, as Forza Horizon 6 is out now.
needs more mercedes (this game was fun..ish, mostly good for driving around mindlessly, FH6 and FH4 are better IMO)

Not as good as 4 or 6 but still worth playing.
Has tons of content, good graphics and performance. 
The story characters are very over the top and insane, and can make you smile. 
Car handling feel better than 6 surprisingly, because of that almost every car feels usable even with stock tuning.

Multiplayer is not worth playing. It feels very unbalanced and people who play dirty get rewarded the most.
Also it's awfull that the ultimate edition does not include all the dlc. On that note the hotwheels expansion is very good and has great tracks.

I love Forza Horizon, i started with 4 and was sold to get 5. If i had the funds or something i would love to promote 6 as well=/.  I believe Forza Horizon is the best Driving/ Racing/ Simulator car game ever made for these reasons. It is realistic and full of different races, maps, terrains just like Grandturismo. Forza horizon allows you to not only customize every car like in the need for speed games from the past but you can fully tune every little thing learning so much and truely getting your vehicle pin point accurate to how you want it to react on the road. The way the cars handle the turns at their high speeds is a lot more realistic feeling/reacting to me than in Grandturismo, when comparing to how it feels driving in real life. I love that you can also free roam forever if you want or get into a specific race style, or even roll next to someone on the road and start a head to head, instead of a game strictly driving just in a race. I can go on and on forever about the amazing graphics, the extra little games and collectibles, the on going cars coming out etc. Forza Horizon is the best and i would love to be reache dout to by the brand and share the love and promotion more and hopefully continue onto horizon 6 and the rest to come. Thank you for everything Forza Horizon =]

I have mixed reactions to this game. First, I like the gameplay as it's typical Forza with arcade driving and plenty of events. But after a while, the game becomes bland because the map is not that interesting compared to other Forza games. The progression is also pretty rough; if you get the Premium Edition, the game spams you with high-end cars and wheelspins, leaving you with nothing to buy or strive for after a while. Don't even get me started on the online racers, as multiplayer is full of rammers. After completing and discovering all the events, the game gets repetitive unless you do online racing or just drive mindlessly around the map.
This game is actually very realistic, the exhaust sound is incredible!

The open world of Mexico is stunning, with diverse biomes like jungles, deserts, beaches, and cities.

Driving feels smooth and responsive, with a huge variety of cars to unlock and customize.

The seasonal changes and dynamic weather keep the world fresh and exciting.

Horizon Festival events are fun, and the mix of races, stunts, and exploration keeps gameplay varied.

Graphics are top-notch, especially with HDR and high settings enabled.

Forza Horizon 5 is one of the best open-world racing games I've played. The graphics are stunning, the map is huge and diverse, and the driving feels both fun and realistic. There are plenty of cars to choose from, exciting events, and lots of activities to keep you entertained for hours. Whether you enjoy casual cruising or competitive racing, Forza Horizon 5 offers an amazing experience. Highly recommended for any racing game fan!

at the start of the game the map and everything looked really cool , after some time it got repetitive and everything was lifeless , the sounds of the cars weren't that good either , i would recommend getting the game if it is at a promotion

I've had this game for so long and haven't played it, but man I've been missing out. This is such a good racing game and lives up to its hype. Personally I wish there were more car choices for tuners but the game is still fun and beautiful.

Fun game, but it would've been better if they’d put a little more thought into the progression system.

With Forza Horizon 6 around the corner, I thought I'd review this. I put a lot of hours in the game and beat the main story relatively early. This is the most played Horizon game I have, with experience dabbling in the previous titles like Horizon 4, 3, and 2 respectively. With that, this isn't my favorite title (it's FH2), but it is a nice one. Better than 4 in my opinion. The game plays how any other FH game would, it seems they don't really change the gameplay aspect, rather than just quality of life changes and the main premise of the story. 5 taking place in Mexico was honestly a breath of fresh air, London felt very cluttered and crowded so being able to take in the beautiful landscape of Mexico was a nice change of pace. The car selection is great as always, with easter eggs like the Halo warthog and such; also featuring my favorite car, the Nissan 350z (2002).
The story was...fine, I guess? The voice acting was horrible but I do like how the narrator says your name, I thought that was cool and to be frank I can't remember if it was in the previous titles. I really liked the Donut missions, as I used to watch Donut a lot and it was fun to play missions that correlated to some of their videos (like Hi and Low). Top Gear is always a banger mission arc as well. It's kinda relaxing going around to find all of the pinatas and smash xp boards and such, a classic Horizon feature.
The DLCs were sort of mid, like the Hot Wheels one was ok and I actually thought the map was pretty cool with the seasons and such, but the other DLC with the teams was boring, I didn't even finish it. I don't think any DLC will top the LEGO one unless 6 pulls out something that correlates to some sort of Japan x Tokyo Drift ♥♥♥♥, now THAT would be insane.
Multiplayer is multiplayer, like the races are fine and so is the "battle royale", there ain't really anything special about it.
It does feel like a complete Horizon game and I enjoyed it for most of the part, but I am only temporarily playing it now just because I'm kinda amped for 6, we will see if it takes the cake. Overall, not a bad game.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pros:
- mexico
- car selection
- scenery is beautiful
- fun side missions and branded missions
- fun off-roading
cons:
- lackluster DLC
- crappy voicelines and cutscenes
- although beautiful, not really interesting POIs
- multiplayer is mid
Great game, very stable and great FPS. runs on extreme settings on my GTX1080 ti.

Not to mention the large variety of cars is also something worth mentioning. I myself enjoyed the rally adventure so much . So yes I recommend this game

The perfect balance of arcade and sim racing.  Be prepare to grind like hell that's the only real con to the game.  Very enjoyable.

The only great part is that the cars now feel heavier which they actually reliesed FH6 and those cars feel probably heavier with the new Japan map but i still dont reccomend buying that either.
Besides buying these FH series games buy The Crew Motorfest trust me, if you want more realism buy assetto corsa.

Good game but not my favorite map

i enjoyed Forza horizon 3 more 
but its a real polished game that has a great list of cars 

just wish there was a better progression instead of giving you a bunch of super cars so easily

Fun game, same as the others, you drive cars around.

Visually good looking, gives me a chance to drive some vehicles I could never afford in real life.. and performs amazing. Never had any issues with these games.

I likes the cars and I likes smashing the XP Boards, reminds me of Tony Hawk in a car.
Very fun game for PC, recommended SIM setup if you are going to play. Nearly unlimited fun, with races, drifting, and stunts! Cross platform compatible, and VERY good graphics.

This is a beautiful game! I would highly recommend it! 
For anyone that is hunting achievements I enjoy the grind for all except the Chicken Dinner achievement, but after learning more about game mechanics it was not that bad! 
Overall the 300+ hours was worth it!
Game is great until it isn't. Infinite loading screen on several occasions.  Reinstalling, change of settings, nothing helped.
there isnt that much content so it gets boring quite fast NOT WORTH THE PRICE
writing this just as FH6 comes out, this was an absolute banger of a game. Lot of people say its worse than some of the other forza games but I dont think so. 9/10 only the storys a bit lacking.

A very fun game to play, nice graphics, but i wanna say it has a bug like when you log-in in into the game you just keep getting stuck on a hot wheels world, idk how to fix this but a really great game to play
Good game. Not the best, and not a simulator but a fun arcade driving game.
One Of The BEST Racing Games I Have Ever Played, Of course had to switch to fh6 but was still a fun game

It's a fun game but the fact that it takes 180GB of space is insane. It ends up hanging out in my external storage and takes so long to move over that I have to plan playing it in advance instead of being something fun I can pick up when I'm in the mood.

I'm sure many people on this platform own enough games that they can't keep them all installed locally. Definitely keep in mind how much space will have to be devoted to this, and how long it will take to reinstall or move from external storage.
